title: Rich Formatting for Form Field Descriptions
excerpt: Native monday.com forms do not support rich formatting for field descriptions, i.e. adding line breaks, numbered instructions, bullet points, external links, etc. My form includes instructions for people to submit inform…

posts.author: mir4bell4 (Mirabella)
posts.cookedHtml:
Native monday.com forms do not support rich formatting for field descriptions, i.e. adding line breaks, numbered instructions, bullet points, external links, etc. My form includes instructions for people to submit information for a new customer, including steps that take place outside of monday.com, such as creating a folder in our Drive, creating a copy of our install sheet template for the customer, etc. The form is meant to be a one-stop-shop for everything a salesperson needs to do to submit a new customer, but the instructions look clunky without line breaks, and I can’t link directly to the external steps.

Rich formatting for the “Add a Description” option for fields on the Form View would essentially accomplish all of my goals. I could list step-by-step instructions where each number has a new line, add links to steps that take place outside of monday.com, and more. I would rather see this feature come to monday.com than use an external form with an integration.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 on this.
The actual way is un profesional for reporting

posts.author: mir4bell4 (Mirabella)
posts.cookedHtml: Don’t forget to vote!

posts.author: mkingsland (Morgan Kingsland)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would also like to request this feature - even just for the ability to link. Specifically; We have a form that requests the user upload a word document that they complete based on a template. We would like to link that template in both the header of the form and in the upload section. In the header, users cannot even copy/paste from the copy, but they should fill out the template prior to completing the rest of the form, so the link is most logical in that place.

Alternately, the ability to add a non-question section to the form that has rich formatting abilities would be amazing.

posts.author: binsento (Vincent)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, please add this !
It’s a big problem for our content team who are struggling with this and I can’t find a work-around.
It seems like such a basic functionality, can’t believe that it’s not possible.

posts.author: ajwhitman (AJ W)
posts.cookedHtml:
I also want this - we use a Form to sign up volunteers, and one of the things we need at the top is for them to read an (external) waiver, and click “I agree”. I sort-of solved this by having the waiver on another page and then linking to the form, but that doesn’t record it in Monday.

I also would like some way to break the Form into sections, with section descriptions, to make it easier to parse while filling it out. Google Forms has something like this. For our Volunteer Sign-up Form, there are different sections for kinds of questions - contact into comes first, but at the end we’re asking them about their skills and I want a way to say “This is the Skill Section. For the following, please set us know how comfortable/confident you are at each of the listed skills.” There’s not really an elegant way to do that without sections and their descriptions.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Until monday releases a richer form builder, we’ve worked with many clients to help them create a solution:

  1. using a monday App for forms (like TypeForm - https://monday.com/marketplace/24); or

  2. having an external form push the submitted data into Integromat or Zapier, and then using that to get it into monday.

Choosing one of these approaches gives you the best of both worlds: A full-featured form builder/component with all the bells and whistles, and your data cleanly imported into monday.

posts.author: aburrell (Aburrell)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, please. It’s crazy you can’t do even basic formatting…

posts.author: Drey (Drey Tengan)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any update on this?

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @mir4bell4, @mkingsland, @ajwhitman and all! We now support links in form descriptions and questions

I’m taking the rest of your feedback and will work on bringing it to the team! Specific use cases about how rich formatting would help you will be most convincing—so feel free to add anything like that on this thread

posts.author: ifigueroa (Insiah Figueroa)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! I saw your reply, but I’m not seeing the ability to add hyperlinks in form descriptions… can you share how this is currently supported? Also, to your second question, this other similar thread has some great use cases (I relate to all of them lol). Would love to hear if there are any new updates on this. Thanks so much!

posts.author: Jih (Ji Hong)
posts.cookedHtml: Would be epic to see some development here. We are a studio with a briefing form built in Monday but we are directing people to go to a folder where the PDF briefing forms of different job types are sitting and getting them to attach it, it would be epic to be able to have the link sitting in the description section. We currently have another URL column to support it but it’s confusing…

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @ifigueroa and @Jih! Thanks for posting

The hyperlink in the form description should look like this:

image1032×410 11.8 KB

If you’re not seeing that, please write in to support@monday.com so we can be sure to get this working for you. I can definitely see how having another URL column to support hyperlinks would be confusing.

@ifigueroa, thanks for linking to that other thread! This is helpful. We are looking into additional formatting options in Q4 of this year, but I’m not sure whether those will look like formatting for the form description and question description fields, or whether they will be formatting options for the way the form as a whole looks.

posts.author: Jih (Ji Hong)
posts.cookedHtml: @Talia thanks for coming back to us! I think what I want is the hyperlink in the form column description… Sorry I didn’t make that clear!

posts.author: chrisclick (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Our team is currently using forms to fill out orders.

We are really lacking by not having a rich text editor on this. As we are having to use a google sheet or attach a word doc, or create the order, then go into the Item itself to edit the update where the text editor does exist.

We really need the ability to edit straight from the form.

Is this something on the roadmap?

posts.author: pjtgrizzly (Reid)
posts.cookedHtml: Rich Text Formatting in workform fields is roughly a must for many scenarios - no unique/weird workflows. Please monday.com! Should be a choice of plain text or rich text fields.

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @pjtgrizzly

Nachliel from Easyapps here

Our Monday form builder app, Easyform, supports HTML in both form and field descriptions.

In addition to that, we offer:

  • Connected Board support:
    • Limiting selection to a single item.
    • Displaying items from multiple connected boards.
    • Limiting select options to items from specific boards where a column is connected to multiple boards.
    • Pro plan feature - item default values support - set default values for connected board columns that are hidden in the form.
  • Upload files to your board’s file columns, with support for:
    • Allowing only specific file types, such as images or pdf.
    • Limiting the number of files that can be uploaded.
    • Setting a maximum for file size.
    • Manually retrying uploads if some or all of them failed.
  • Set default values for the response item’s hidden group, status, connected board, and dropdown columns. To do so, select Item default values from the Settings menu.
  • Add the item’s group as just another field to the form.
  • Control the page’s top margin with the ability to set different values for desktop and mobile. To do so, select Page from the Settings menu.

If you’re interested in creating styled forms that better match your brand and embed perfectly on your web page, you can check out our Monday.com app Easyform and our extensive documentation site.

You can set up a free demo here or contact us at support@easyappshq.com.

Here is an example of our form in action:

I hope this helps,

Nachliel

posts.author: jstokes (Joanne Stokes)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any update on this feature request? We have supporting documents that people could view if they are struggling to decide their answer to a question. I would like them to be able to click on that in the description to open a new tab instead of copying the URL and pasting in a new tab.

There are also situations where turning a word or phrase a color or making it bold to ensure it stands out would improve the data collection process.

posts.author: remkosignassist (RemkoVermeulen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Very surprised you do not support richt text fields. Airtable has been doing so from the start and for me not having this features has lead to quite some complications:

  • Our Development team doesn’t want to use Monday as they can’t do Code formatting
  • Our test team doesn’t want to use Monday as they can not add screenshots to the description (separate as a file yes, but does not give a clear description and not user friendly)
  • Our PM can not add a description field for each user story, so has to use the work around to create an update (which support richt text ?!) and pin it to the top.

posts.author: PM_Karen (Karen H)
posts.cookedHtml:
@moday is there any update on rich formatting in Forms?
-I need to be able to italicize my company name in a form description field and unable to do so.
-I also need the ability to give a URL a title to clean up.

posts.author: HPETRIE (Hannah Petrie)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com do you have an update on this?

posts.author: DeeMc (Dee Mc)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, Is there a way to collapse the form field question description or make it into an information bubble? Some of mine can be lengthy which causes scrolling on the board. It would be great to have this feature.

posts.author: mmartin3 (Meagan)
posts.cookedHtml: Seconding this feature request — rich text or markdown would be incredible to help emphasize information in workform instructions when completing forms.

posts.author: WLane (Wally Lane)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes this would be great. Many users copy and paste and of course it loses the formatting.

posts.author: ShelleyDonovan (Shelley Donovan Ritchie)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any update on this?

posts.author: kevin_vaghasiya (kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
For anyone still looking for a solution here, I switched to FormFlow and it has rich text blocks that support formatted text, images, links, the works. You just drag them into the form wherever you need instructions or context.

Way more flexible than what native WorkForms gives you for descriptions.
